src/main/java/top/rslly/iot/config/Secur.general_idea: Replace the deprecated @EnableGlobalMethodSecurity with the Spring Security 6+ standard @EnableMethodSecurity.
Migrate HttpSecurity configuration from the deprecated fluent API (antMatchers, .and()) to the new lambda-based API (requestMatchers, .authorizeHttpRequests).
Update CORS configuration to use the new lambda-based setup and replace deprecated applyPermitDefaultValues with explicit allowed origin patterns.
Replace direct BCryptPasswordEncoder instantiation with PasswordEncoderFactories.createDelegatingPasswordEncoder for better compatibility and standard practice.
Add necessary imports for the new Spring Security 6 APIs and configuration classes.
Add Apache License 2.0 header to the source file.
Explicitly add @Configuration annotation to the class, although often implied by @EnableWebSecurity, it is good practice in newer Spring versions.
Update WebSecurityCustomizer to use requestMatchers instead of the deprecated antMatchers.
Ensure the DaoAuthenticationProvider is explicitly configured with the password encoder bean.
